intro: The clearest split is that 1Password carries medium lock-in and AWS Secrets Manager carries low lock-in. 1Password is Secrets Management tool for developers; AWS Secrets Manager is Managed secrets service rotating RDS, Redshift, and custom credentials with KMS encryption and IAM. The data points 1Password at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and AWS Secrets Manager at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Pricing: 1Password is subscription from $2.99; AWS Secrets Manager is subscription from $0.02. Lock-in is medium for 1Password and low for AWS Secrets Manager, with DX at 4/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 4/5. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits solo, static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: 1Password is for teams; AWS Secrets Manager is for teams; decide based on how much lock-in you tolerate.
verdict_a: Choose 1Password if you can accept medium lock-in in exchange for scope and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.
verdict_b: Choose AWS Secrets Manager if you prefer low lock-in and easier exit and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.

faq.q: Is 1Password cheaper than AWS Secrets Manager?
faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $2.99 for 1Password and $0.02 for AWS Secrets Manager. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from 1Password to AWS Secrets Manager?
faq.a: 1Password is tagged with medium lock-in (items exportable to 1pux/json but deeply integrated workflows). AWS Secrets Manager is tagged with low lock-in (aws-bound secrets). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, 1Password or AWS Secrets Manager?
faq.a: Both are rated 4/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is AWS Secrets Manager a good alternative to 1Password?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches teams, since both list the same fit. AWS Secrets Manager is flagged as not for solo, static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
